spec.name: turbo_widgets showcase app + dev-conventions source refactor
scope: Part A: Refactor turbo_widgets/lib/src so the source tree conforms to dev-conventions/folders-and-file-types.md, using the five use-case aisles as real domain folders.
Part B: Transform turbo_widgets/example into a live, interactive shop-metaphor showcase built on top of the refactored library.
spec.purpose: Turn the example into a live, interactive demonstration styled as a fake shop app where the widgets themselves are the products. Along the way, restructure the library source so the actual folder layout matches the aisles the visitor browses through.

metaphor.name: Turbo Widgets Shop
metaphor.core_idea: Every public turbo_widgets export is a 'product' sold in the shop. Browsing the shop naturally exercises the layout, navigation, and collection widgets. Opening a product opens a widget detail view where visitors play with debug controls and watch the live preview update.
metaphor.double_duty: Widgets that appear as shop chrome (TContextualAppBar, TContextualBottomNavigation, TContextualSideNavigation, TCategorySection, TCollectionSection, TCollectionToolbar, TFeatureCard, TBentoGrid, TContextualButtons, ContextualButtonsProvider, TResponsiveBuilder, TViewBuilder) are demonstrated twice: once as the real running chrome of the shop, and once as products with their own detail pages and debug controls.

primary_audience: Developers integrating turbo_widgets into their own apps
Maintainers regression-checking widget visuals after changes

must_use: turbo_notifiers (TNotifier-based reactive cells; multi-listenable builders where applicable)
may_use: Other turbo_ packages only when a widget directly requires them (e.g. turbo_forms is already a transitive dep of turbo_widgets via TFormSection)
must_not: Add Firebase, networking, or any backend dependencies
Pull in unrelated turbo_ packages just to namedrop them
Use Material AppBar/Drawer/BottomNavigationBar where a turbo_widgets equivalent exists
Hold business state inside views — all mutable showcase state lives in view models on TNotifier cells
Hardcode mock data inside views — mock data lives in dedicated services exposed via DI
convention_anchors: dev-conventions/folders-and-file-types: domain-driven src layout, concept folders, one type per file, snake_case files for Flutter
dev-conventions/mvvm: View → ViewModel → Service → API; views are stateless delegators
dev-conventions/views-and-view-models: stateless views, view models own all screen state, reactive cells exposed read-only
dev-conventions/state: TNotifier-style cells; busy/error/empty handled at presentation controller; empty is in-view content
CLAUDE.md (root): naming TPrefix for turbo types, ViewModels suffixed ViewModel, services suffixed Service

widget_inventory._note: Every entry must appear at least once in the running showcase. The 'demo_strategy' field is the autonomous default and may be revised by user answers.

cards.demo_strategy: Grid of feature cards on the showcase home view

category.demo_strategy: Header above one category section demo
Two demos: horizontal layout and grid layout, both with show-all behavior toggled by maxItems

collection.demo_strategy: Rendered as items inside collection sections
Used as item builder for the list-layout collection demo
Header banner above one collection section demo
One demo per layout enum value: bento, list, grid (cycled live via TCollectionToolbar)
Mounted above the collection section demo, wired to view model notifiers for searchQuery, sortValue, filterValue, layout

detail.demo_strategy: Top of a detail view that also hosts TFormSection and TKeyValueField rows
Metadata row inside the detail header and inside read-only metadata blocks
Two cards: one driven by formConfig (TFormConfig from turbo_forms with mixed field types), one driven by children (custom shadcn_ui widgets)

forms_interactive.demo_strategy: Dedicated 'Interactive Form' showcase view that walks through a multi-step flow exercising all 3 step types
Owned by the showcase view model, disposed in onModelDispose
Step 1 of the interactive form: name + email
Step 2: pick a plan via TCardOption list
Used by the card selection step

layout.demo_strategy: Standalone demo with controls to mutate item sizes and switch animation enum (none/fade/scale/slide) live via view model TNotifier<TBentoGridAnimation>
Built from the bento demo view model state
Wraps a content area whose item count is mutated by buttons to visualize height scaling

misc.demo_strategy: Two collapsible sections on a misc view, expansion state on view model TNotifier<bool>
Renders inline asset markdown content, onOpen pushes a snackbar via shadcn_ui
Used as the view model host for the showcase shell view AND for at least one detail view that pushes contextual buttons
Toggle button reveals/hides content with vertical shrink
Toggle button reveals/hides content with horizontal shrink

navigation.demo_strategy: Pushed as TContextualButtonsConfig.top from showcase view models
Pushed as TContextualButtonsConfig.bottom on mobile; switching tab is the primary nav action
Used inside contextual app bar/bottom nav builders directly
Standalone demo view that lets the user toggle isExpanded and layout enum (auto/vertical/horizontal)

contextual.demo_strategy: Provided once at the app shell via TViewBuilder.hostContextualButtons=true
Wraps the app at root with a Map<ShowcaseRoute, TViewButtonsConfig<...>> covering every showcase route
Each showcase route defines mobile + desktop builders so the contextual nav swap is visible by resizing the window
Singleton consumed by TContextualButtons; not registered separately

responsive.demo_strategy: Wraps the showcase shell so device type drives contextual nav placement; also showcased on a dedicated 'Responsive' view that prints current TData and TTools values live
Default config used at root; the responsive showcase view exposes the active values
Read inside the responsive showcase view via the TResponsiveBuilder data parameter

library_refactor._anchor: dev-conventions/folders-and-file-types.md — single core/ plus one folder per domain; each domain uses shared/ (+ optional feature/) subtrees; files only inside named concept folders.
principles: Each aisle from navigation_structure.aisles becomes a real domain folder at lib/src/{domain}/
Each domain has a shared/ subtree (no features) unless a widget bundle naturally fits as a feature folder (the interactive form is the only such case).
Inside shared/ and each feature/, only concept folders (widgets/, models/, enums/, services/, abstracts/, mixins/, utils/, config/, extensions/, typedefs/, constants/).
No core/ folder — nothing in the current codebase is shared across domains, so core/ would be empty and is therefore omitted.
One type per file, snake_case filenames, PascalCase types — already matches the current repo.

The package facade lib/turbo_widgets.dart is rewritten with the new export paths. External consumers importing package:turbo_widgets/turbo_widgets.dart are unaffected; internal relative imports are all rewritten.

widgets/: t_view_builder.dart  // moved from misc/ — its purpose is MVVM host + contextual buttons integration

lib/src/detail_and_forms/interactive_for._note: Feature folder inside the detail_and_forms domain — the interactive form is a cohesive bundle with its own controller, step configs, and renderers.

widgets/: t_vertical_shrink.dart  // split out of the current t_shrink.dart
t_horizontal_shrink.dart // split out of the current t_shrink.dart

deletions: lib/src/enums/ (contents split across navigation/shared/enums and collections/shared/enums)
lib/src/models/ (contents split across navigation/shared/models and collections/shared/models)
lib/src/responsive/ (contents moved to utilities/shared/ concept folders)
lib/src/widgets/ (all subfolders collapse into the five domain folders)
file_renames: lib/src/widgets/misc/t_shrink.dart → split into layout_and_motion/shared/widgets/t_vertical_shrink.dart and t_horizontal_shrink.dart (one type per file rule)
library_refactor.public_facade: lib/turbo_widgets.dart is rewritten to export the new paths. The exported type symbols remain identical — only the internal file paths change.
library_refactor.internal_imports: All relative + package: imports inside the moved files are updated to the new paths.
assumption_check_required: TInteractiveForm feature folder placement inside detail_and_forms — confirm this is the right domain vs a standalone 'forms' domain.
TViewBuilder placement in navigation/ (not utilities/) because its purpose is coupling TBaseViewModel + contextual buttons hosting.
Splitting t_shrink.dart into two files to enforce one-type-per-file — confirm this is acceptable as a breaking file-path change for internal users.

root_widget_chain: TResponsiveBuilder(builder: (context, child, constraints, tools, data) => ShowcaseShellView(deviceType: data.deviceType))

responsibilities: Renders the active showcase view inside an IndexedStack/Navigator-style switcher
Pushes a TContextualButtonsConfig per route through ContextualButtonsProvider lookup, splitting nav vs page chrome by device type

folder_layout._anchor: dev-conventions/folders-and-file-types — domain-driven, concept folders only, snake_case files
tree: services/mock_data_service.dart                      (provides categories, collection items, plans, calendar dates)
globals/dependency_injection.dart                    (GetIt-free composition root that hands services to view models)
widgets/showcase_section.dart                        (small reusable wrapper card around each in-view widget demo)
feature/collection/views/collection_showcase/        (uses TCollectionToolbar bound to view model notifiers)
rules: Each view colocated with its view model under views/{view-name}/
Mock data is a service, never inlined in views or view models
Every showcase screen is a stateless TViewBuilder<XxxShowcaseViewModel>
View model extends TBaseViewModel and exposes all mutable state via TNotifier cells
Views consume notifiers via ValueListenableBuilder / ValueListenableBuilderX2..X6 / MultiListenableBuilder from turbo_notifiers
Each view defines its contextualButtonsBuilder inside TViewBuilder, returning TContextualButtonsConfig with appbar/bottom/side as appropriate
Initialise resolves the mock data service and primes notifiers; dispose tears down controllers

ShopHomeViewModel: derived featuredProducts via ValueListenableBuilderX4 over the above
ThemeService: Singleton ValueListenable<Brightness> brightness, toggled by the app bar action. Injected into ShadApp at the root.

services: ShowcaseCatalogService — provides the full list of ShowcaseEntry(widget name, aisleKey, tagline, route, productViewBuilder, thumbnailBuilder)
MockContentService — provides generic placeholder content used by product previews (sample names, descriptions, metadata rows, markdown snippets). No invented business concepts.
ThemeService — owns a ValueListenable<Brightness> toggled by the app bar theme action
TContextualButtonsService — existing turbo_widgets singleton, consumed via TViewBuilder
product_detail_page_structure: TViewBuilder<XxxProductViewModel> at root, contextualButtonsBuilder returns a TContextualButtonsConfig with TContextualAppBar on top (leading = back, title = widget name, actions = [theme toggle]) and the aisle nav shortcuts on bottom (mobile) / left (desktop)
1. TDetailHeader (widget name + tagline, no save button)
2. Wrap of TKeyValueField rows showing metadata: Aisle, Source path (post-refactor), Stateful/Stateless, Direct dependencies
3. Live preview panel — renders the widget with current TNotifier values via ValueListenableBuilder/MultiListenableBuilder
4. TFormSection 'Debug controls' with shadcn_ui fields bound to the view model's notifiers (one control per tunable prop)
5. TCollapsibleSection 'Advanced' (collapsed by default) with the rarely-changed props
6. TMarkdownFileItem 'Usage snippet' rendering the current Dart code that produces the preview

top_level_routes: shop_home — store front hero + every aisle section + featured list
aisle_detail — listing of all products in a single aisle
product_detail — live showcase for one widget with debug controls

aisles.label: Navigation
aisles.tagline: App shells, tab bars, side rails, and the contextual button system

aisles.label: Collections
aisles.tagline: Cards, lists, bento grids, and everything that turns a list of stuff into a browsable shelf

aisles.label: Detail & Forms
aisles.tagline: Detail pages, forms, and multi-step flows

aisles.label: Layout & Motion
aisles.tagline: Shrinks, calculated heights, and the little helpers that make UIs feel alive

aisles.label: Utilities
aisles.tagline: Building blocks: view builders, responsive helpers, markdown previews

shell_chrome.top: TContextualAppBar pushed per route; title reflects current shop area ('Turbo Widgets Shop' on home, '{Aisle Label}' on aisle detail, '{Widget Name}' on product detail). Leading is a back button on detail routes.
shell_chrome.mobile_nav: TContextualBottomNavigation holding one tab per aisle (5 tabs). Selecting a tab routes to that aisle detail.
shell_chrome.desktop_nav: TContextualSideNavigation on the left holding the same 5 aisle tabs plus a 'Shop' home shortcut above them, swapped in via TResponsiveBuilder device type.

types_without_product_pages.rule: Only visible Widget subclasses become products. Enums, configs, controllers, abstracts, mixins, models, and extensions are documented inside the relevant widget's product page — as debug-control sources, metadata rows, or markdown snippets — not as separate products.
examples: TBentoGridAnimation enum → dropdown control on TBentoGrid page
TContextualPosition / TContextualAllowFilter enums → controls on TContextualButtons page
TInteractiveFormController → owned by the TInteractiveForm page's view model
TCardOption / TTextInputStepConfig / TCardSelectionStepConfig / TCalendarStepConfig → used inside the TInteractiveForm page demo
TButtonConfig → shared data structure for every Navigation aisle product
TBreakpointConfig / TData / TTools / TDeviceType / TOrientation → displayed live on the TResponsiveBuilder page

ui_patterns.shop_home_layout: Hero banner using TCollectionHeader ('Turbo Widgets Shop — every Flutter widget you didn't know you needed'). Below it a TCategorySection per widget category showing TCategoryCard items. Below that a TCollectionSection titled 'Featured' rendering TCollectionCard items via bento layout, with a TCollectionToolbar above it that the user can actually use to search/sort/filter/swap layout of the featured list.
ui_patterns.category_detail_layout: TCategoryHeader banner, then a TCollectionSection listing every widget in that category. Layout switchable via TCollectionToolbar. Tapping an item opens the product detail route.
ui_patterns.product_detail_layout: TDetailHeader with widget name + one-line tagline. A row of TKeyValueField metadata (category, source file, animation/layout enum if applicable, required deps). Below: a large live preview panel rendering the widget. Below the preview: a TFormSection 'Debug controls' that exposes every tunable prop as a ShadCheckbox / ShadSelect / ShadInput / ShadSlider bound to the view model's TNotifier cells — changes update the preview immediately. Advanced props live in a TCollapsibleSection. A TMarkdownFileItem below the controls shows the widget's current Dart usage snippet.
ui_patterns.interactivity: Every debug control is bound to a TNotifier<T> on the product detail view model. The live preview is rendered by a ValueListenableBuilder (or ValueListenableBuilderX2..X6 / MultiListenableBuilder when multiple cells feed one render).
ui_patterns.empty_states: Category and collection listings include an in-page placeholder when a search filter empties the list, following dev-conventions/state.md (empty state = in-place content, not error).

mock_data._status: TBD — themed vs neutral pending user answer

non_goals: Custom theming beyond default ShadThemeData light/dark
Tests (the example is not under coverage)
Cart, checkout, favorites, purchase, wishlist, or any fake commerce functionality beyond what the widgets themselves provide
Any invented business concept that is not backed by an existing turbo_widgets export

extensibility.new_widget_playbook: The example directory ships a README.md at example/README.md that documents the exact steps to add a new product page when a new widget is added to turbo_widgets. Steps: (1) add the widget to the showcase catalog service, (2) create example/lib/showcase/feature/{category}/views/{widget_name}_showcase/ with view + view model, (3) register the view in the router, (4) add the product card to the category section. The README is the source of truth for keeping the showcase in lockstep with the widget library.

decisions.decision: With exploration — read relevant widget source files between questions
decisions.source: User selection on initial research-mode question

decisions.topic: App shell uses contextual navigation widgets as the real chrome
decisions.decision: Yes — eat-your-own-dogfood. The shell wraps every showcase view with TContextualButtons supplied through ContextualButtonsProvider; nav placement (bottom on mobile, side on desktop) is driven by TResponsiveBuilder.
decisions.source: Autonomous: turbo_widgets exports a complete contextual nav system explicitly designed to be the app shell. Choosing anything else would leave the most central widgets undemonstrated in their intended role.

decisions.decision: Every showcase view is a stateless TViewBuilder<XxxShowcaseViewModel>; mutable state lives on TNotifier cells inside view models.
decisions.source: Autonomous: dev-conventions/views-and-view-models + state.md mandate this; turbo_widgets ships TViewBuilder for exactly this case.

decisions.decision: Domain-driven src layout with core/ + shell/ + showcase/ feature folders; one type per file; snake_case filenames per Flutter convention.
decisions.source: Autonomous: dev-conventions/folders-and-file-types

decisions.decision: All mock data lives in core/services/mock_data_service.dart and is injected into view models via constructors.
decisions.source: Autonomous: dev-conventions/mvvm + state.md prohibit views/view models from owning canonical data.

decisions.decision: Add explicit dependencies on turbo_forms (already a transitive of turbo_widgets via TFormSection) and align all turbo_* version constraints with the workspace versions; keep shadcn_ui pinned to ^0.53.5 to match the parent package.
decisions.source: Autonomous: TFormSection imports package:turbo_forms/turbo_forms.dart directly, so the example must declare it explicitly to avoid relying on a transitive.

decisions.decision: Fake shop app where every turbo_widgets export is a product. Shop chrome is built from the turbo_widgets navigation/collection/category widgets. Opening a product opens a live showcase with debug controls that mutate the widget's input parameters in real time.
decisions.source: User: 'make it a fake shop app where the widgets are the products themselves and opening them will showcase the widget with debug controls that change its input parameters'

decisions.decision: None. The shop is a pure browse-and-open catalog in shop clothing. No add-to-cart, no favorites, no checkout, no fake commerce anywhere. TInteractiveForm gets its own product detail page like every other widget.
decisions.source: User: 'just make it with the widgets we have now - dont create anything yet we dont have'

decisions.topic: Keeping the showcase in sync with new widgets
decisions.decision: example/README.md documents the add-a-new-product playbook (catalog service entry, new showcase view/view-model folder, router registration, category card entry). The README is the source of truth for future contributors.
decisions.source: User: 'make it apparent in the readme for any new widgets to be added'

decisions.decision: Restructure turbo_widgets/lib/src so the folder tree conforms to dev-conventions/folders-and-file-types.md. The five aisles from decision 8 become real domain folders (navigation/, collections/, detail_and_forms/, layout_and_motion/, utilities/). Each uses a shared/ subtree; the interactive form becomes a feature folder inside detail_and_forms. No core/ folder (nothing is truly cross-domain).
decisions.source: User: 'we must also refactor the locations of our files and conform to the folder conventions of dev-conventions skill and make those categories you suggested actual folders'

decisions.decision: TViewBuilder lives in navigation/shared/widgets/ because its sole purpose is coupling turbo_mvvm's TViewModelBuilder with the contextual buttons overlay system (it imports TContextualButtonsService directly). It is not a generic utility.
decisions.source: Autonomous — based on reading lib/src/widgets/misc/t_view_builder.dart which imports TContextualButtonsService and TContextualButtonsConfig directly.

decisions.topic: Interactive form as a feature folder
decisions.decision: TInteractiveForm + its controller, step configs, and renderers become a feature folder detail_and_forms/interactive_form/ instead of cluttering the domain's shared/ subtree. Every other detail/form widget stays in detail_and_forms/shared/widgets/.
decisions.source: Autonomous — dev-conventions permits feature folders inside a domain, and the interactive form is a self-contained bundle distinct from the plain detail widgets.

decisions.decision: Split lib/src/widgets/misc/t_shrink.dart (which declares both TVerticalShrink and THorizontalShrink) into two files in layout_and_motion/shared/widgets/, enforcing the one-type-per-file rule.
decisions.source: Autonomous — dev-conventions/folders-and-file-types: 'Each class or top-level type is in its own file whenever practical.'

decisions.decision: Match the aisle labels exactly with snake_case: navigation/, collections/, detail_and_forms/, layout_and_motion/, utilities/. Two-word names are kept verbatim.
decisions.source: User selection on domain-names question.

decisions.decision: The top TContextualAppBar on every route includes a sun/moon action that flips ShadApp between its light and dark ShadThemeData. Theme state is owned by a ThemeService registered at app start and exposed via ValueListenable<Brightness>.
decisions.source: User selection on theme-toggle question.

decisions.decision: Hybrid approach. Visual widgets (cards, nav bars, bento, shrinks, forms, detail header, collapsible, markdown) render a tiny live miniature of themselves inside TCollectionCard-shaped wrappers on the listings. Structural/non-visual widgets (TContextualButtons, ContextualButtonsProvider, TViewBuilder, TResponsiveBuilder) fall back to a Lucide icon + name + tagline layout. Both paths render inside TCollectionCard so the listing is uniform.
decisions.source: Autonomous — balances honesty (real widgets) with feasibility (some widgets have no visual).

decisions.decision: Shop home starts with a TCollectionHeader hero ('Turbo Widgets Shop' title, short tagline), followed by a TCategorySection listing the five aisles, followed by a 'Featured' TCollectionSection rendering every widget in a bento layout with a functional TCollectionToolbar (working search by widget name, sort by name/aisle, filter by aisle, layout switch between bento/list/grid).
decisions.source: Autonomous — exercises the maximum number of collection/category widgets on a single screen.

decisions.decision: Product detail pages expose one debug control per publicly-tunable widget parameter that has a meaningful user-facing effect. Skip internal/layout-only props (borderRadius, padding, spacing) unless the widget has nothing else to vary. Callback props are wired to a snackbar toast so visitors see the callback fire. Every control is bound to a TNotifier<T> on the product detail view model, never local setState.
decisions.source: Autonomous — aligns with dev-conventions/state and keeps debug panels useful without overwhelming the visitor.

decisions.decision: The shop home and aisle detail TCollectionToolbar instances actually filter their lists — search matches widget name or tagline (case-insensitive substring), sort cycles name/aisle, filter narrows by aisle, layout flips the section's layout enum. No fake/decorative interactions.
decisions.source: Autonomous — decision 8 requires only existing widgets, and a working search is still pure widget usage, not invented commerce.

decisions.decision: All icons come from shadcn_ui's bundled LucideIcons set. No custom assets, no new icon packages.
decisions.source: Autonomous — shadcn_ui is already a dependency and LucideIcons covers every widget concept.
